response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an experienced full-stack and backend software developer with a strong web and distributed systems background, but the Principal Performance Architect role requires deep hardware-level expertise — SoC/IP modeling, GPU architecture, SIMD/MIMD architectures, and deep-learning workload optimization — none of which appear anywhere in his resume. His closest relevant experience is low-latency asynchronous I/O work at Dunnhumby and some AI-agent tooling at re:cinq, but these are several abstraction layers removed from the silicon-level performance modeling and hardware-software co-design this role demands.
strengths: Long tenure (~17 years) with demonstrated progression to staff-level engineering roles
Experience with low-latency, high-throughput systems (millions of HTTP req/s at Dunnhumby using async I/O and high-performance data structures)
Exposure to AI-agent infrastructure and tooling at re:cinq, showing some familiarity with the AI ecosystem
concerns: No SoC or IP modeling experience — the core technical requirement of the role
No GPU architecture, SIMD/MIMD, or hardware-software co-design background anywhere in 17 years of work history
No deep learning or neural network workload analysis experience; AI exposure is limited to application-layer agent frameworks

key_factors.factor: No SoC/IP modeling or hardware architecture experience
No GPU or AI accelerator workload expertise
Strong low-latency systems and performance engineering background
